screen_capacity.claim_boundary: capacity metadata for finite screen diagnostics; patch_count is a regulator size unless the run explicitly declares physical-cell toy mode

public_reference.claim_boundary: Public-source OPH CMB-note values imported for context. They are not official Planck likelihoods and are not finite-screen simulator derivations.

screen_power_fit.claim_boundary: Finite screen C_l power-law diagnostic. eta_R is estimated from the simulator field and compared with the Planck scalar-tilt target, but this is not a Planck likelihood and not a physical CMB prediction.

finite_screen_capacity_signatures.claim_boundary: not yet a detected cutoff unless spectra show stable finite-N scaling

claim_boundary: Finite observer-screen CMB-anomaly diagnostic from run C_l receipts. It can test whether screen synchronization leaves low-ell, parity, large-angle, and finite-capacity imprints. It is not a Planck likelihood, not a Boltzmann acoustic transfer, not a physical CMB prediction, and not proof of 3D bulk emergence.
